mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and eases financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the policy path is still declining.
failure_condition: Reject this hypothesis if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration, higher recession risk, and weaker earnings expectations, which should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next month once the unemployment rate jumps above its recent trend.
failure_condition: Reject this hypothesis if triggered observations do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 0.5 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: When RSASFS and PAYEMS both strengthen together, household demand and payroll income are improving simultaneously, supporting revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject this hypothesis if the joint positive-change regime in RSAFS and PAYEMS fails to deliver positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of instances, or if the interaction term is not statistically positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and better cyclical growth momentum, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings revisions improve.
failure_condition: Reject this hypothesis if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods with rising INDPRO, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below trend signals housing-demand weakness and a slowing construction pipeline, which should weigh on cyclical earnings and make S&P 500 returns negative over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject this hypothesis if sub-trend HOUST periods are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS stays low, real policy rates fall and financial conditions loosen, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS
regime_hint: inflation rising but policy still accommodative
failure_condition: Reject this hypothesis if the CPIAUCSL-minus-FEDFUNDS spread trigger does not precede positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening labor market, falling UNRATE alongside rising PAYEMS signals broad hiring strength and income support, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month through better consumption and earnings prospects.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines and PAYEMS rises over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject this hypothesis if the joint labor-improvement trigger does not yield positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of observations, or if the effect disappears when either proxy is removed.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle slowdown, weakening GDPC1 together with soft RSAFS marks broad demand deceleration and lower sales momentum, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Reject this hypothesis if joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not correspond to negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not significantly below zero.
